title: Document .tick/config.md convention and wire it into the runner
description:
Implement spec §5 (docs/design/execution-model-v2-epic-chaining-and-learning-loop.md). Touches SKILL.md and claude-runner.md, so it runs after the other ticks editing those files.

Define the convention — .tick/config.md, a markdown file with three recognized sections:
- Testing: exact test commands, incl. surgical per-package invocations.
- Environment: pre-flight checks run once before the first wave (CLI tools present, services up). Rule: test, don't ask.
- Rules: project-specific constraints for implementers.
Read fresh at point of use (same by-reference rule as .tick/learnings.md). Fallback when absent: current behavior (implementers discover test commands themselves).

Changes:
1. skills/ticks/SKILL.md: document the convention where .tick/ contents are discussed (near the git-tracking prerequisite), incl. why it is not CLAUDE.md (config.md is the contract for dispatched implementers specifically).
2. skills/ticks/references/claude-runner.md: orchestrator reads it at run start and runs the Environment pre-flight before launching wave 1; implementer prompt template Instructions gain 'Read .tick/config.md (if present) for test commands and project rules' next to the learnings instruction added by an earlier tick (it will already be in the template when this tick runs).
acceptance_criteria: Convention documented in SKILL.md with all 3 sections and fallback; claude-runner.md has the run-start read + pre-flight step; implementer template instructs reading .tick/config.md
